---
title: "Replicating Published SEMs"
subtitle: "Using lavaan and OpenMx"
---

The findings of published Structural Equation Model analyses can be replicated if authors make their data available. The data can be in the form of raw data or in the form of summary data (correlations, variances, covariances, means, and standard deviations). Either way, it doesn't matter. Standard SEM software can use either format to run analyses. This site shows how to replicate published findings (or not as the case may be) using [lavaan](https://www.lavaan.ugent.be/) and [OpenMx](https://openmx.ssri.psu.edu/).

Copying correlations, covariances, means, and standard deviations can be a tedious and error-prone process, especially when the model contains a large number of variables. A section of this site shows how to extract data from PDF files using the **R** packages [pdftools](https://docs.ropensci.org/pdftools/) and [tabulapdf](https://docs.ropensci.org/tabulapdf/index.html).

All the analyses presented here show a path diagram. There are **R** packages that can be used to draw path diagrams; indeed, there are packages that can draw path diagrams given the model, but I like the precision and control afforded by [Ti*k*Z](https://tikz.dev/), a $\mathsf{\LaTeX}$ package. A section of this site presents **Ti*k*Z** scripts to draw the path diagrams.
